title: excoecaria lanceolaria
intro: Excoecaria lanceolaria, also known as the poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Excoecaria lanceolaria include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxic sap, which can cause skin irritation and other adverse effects.
